sections: P6 — Run the full implementation proof wall

repo_truth: The clean-LF validator rejects the already executed review-1 dispatch because its 53 paths were locale-sorted rather than ordinal-sorted. The dispatch is immutable and its reviewer admitted the declared aggregate. GitNexus reports validate_subject_manifest CRITICAL with 115 impacted, 53 direct, and 18 processes, though unrelated Rust graph edges visibly pollute that count. The repair authorizes only one filename/raw-SHA ordering admission; uniqueness, entry hashes, aggregate, schema, and all other dispatches stay strict.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the two staged-blob hashes plus aggregate before reviewing.
Review only whether the exact-byte ordering admission is the smallest safe repair for the immutable executed dispatch.
Check that changed/unknown bytes refuse and no other validator contract is relaxed.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence and smallest in-scope remediation.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, handoff/ledger writes, or user task hops.
No reordering or mutation of the executed dispatch and no broad validator exception.
No production runtime, definitions, schemas, Cargo, pipeline/stage, SDK, or HCM-2.2+ work.
tasks: Invoke the ordered required skills and return BLOCKED on any subject mismatch.
Verify the validator failure and immutable dispatch ordering defect from live bytes.
Verify the packet repair permits only an exact filename/SHA ordering admission while retaining every other check.
Return CLEAN only with no Critical or Required finding; otherwise CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if staged-blob fingerprint admission or authority cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED instead of authorizing dispatch mutation or a non-byte-bound exception.
